channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Broken glass, 11th March
item.description:
Lots of broken glass, including small and large shards covering areas of footpath en route to primary School
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Magpie Hall Lane, Bromley Common, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flooding, 11th March
item.description:
Blocked drain at junction of CHurch Court and Rotherhithe Street causing regular flooding

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Near the bus shelter, 11th March
item.description:
There are numerous potholes along Raydon street, causing the whole building to shake when a car or bus drives over them. This has been ongoing for several months.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Raydon Street, Dartmouth Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The beeps do not work, 11th March
item.description:
When it is time to cross the road the lights do not beep to let blind people know it is time to cross. There are at least two blind people on one of the roads nearby who use this crossing frequently and they struggle as a result. The noise should be activated to ensure that we are not wrongly putting people at risk
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elmers End Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish and dog poo along street, 11th March
item.description:
Everyday there is a see of rubbish both on the pavement and in road along kerb as well as dog poo. The school kids have to dodge this kind of stuff everyday.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Felmingham Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned/Stored Bicycles on Footway, 11th March
item.description:
4–5 bicycles have been chained here for 12+ months, obstructing the pavement forcing buggies/wheelchairs onto the mud. Although the bikes never move, the covers were replaced. This appears to be unauthorised long-term storage of potentially stolen property

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lowestoft Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned/Stored Bicycles on Footway, 11th March
item.description:
4–5 bicycles have been chained here for 12+ months, obstructing the pavement forcing buggies/wheelchairs onto the mud. Although the bikes never move, the covers were replaced. This appears to be unauthorised long-term storage of potentially stolen property

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lowestoft Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: On the corner of Warren Street and Cleveland Street, roughly opposite 37 Warren St, 11th March
item.description:
I moved into an office at 37 Warren St last April. Since that time, this bicycle has been locked to this bike rack and has never moved. It is taking up space and could be donated to a bike repair charity.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 128-134, Cleveland Street, Fitzrovia, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Yellow lines were not finished off on the street of Legaat Road and the semi circle corner of Shawbrooke Rd, 11th March
item.description:
Accidents occuring becuase people leaving their car parked on the corners of these two street.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Shawbrooke Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cardboard boxes, plastic and long wooden posts dumped, 11th March
item.description:
Several Cardboard boxes, plastic and long wooden posts dumped at base of substation steps.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lea Bridge Service Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: At the bottom of Broomsleigh Street, 11th March
item.description:
Its very dark at the bottom of Broomsleigh Street, where it connects to the Black Path. It seems street lights have gone out here and feels unsafe at night!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Broomsleigh Street, West Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping continues, 11th March
item.description:
Card board that can recycled dumped by local residents with other household rubbish including an envelope an address from a restaurant business on Denmark Hill. This has been there for 2 weeks now and of course idiots passing by add more to the pile. It's now on a public pathway please get rid of it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pitman Street, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multi cars parked on double yellow blips driveway, 11th March
item.description:
Multiple cars parked in double yellow with blips block access to driveway used by four garages
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Manhole cover broken on footpath inbetween number 9 and number 11, 11th March
item.description:
Manhole cover broken on footpath inbetween number 9 and number 11 The Chine, N21 School nearby therefore safety hazard, needs to be repaired asap.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): The Chine, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped bags of rubbish, 11th March
item.description:
Dumped bags of rubbish not cleared for over 2 months. Over the weeks more bags dumped at same location next to railings. Railings are next to house that has a fridge in the front garden for about 6 months. Council's weekly cleanse is missing clearing the rubbish.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Battery Road, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Zebra crossing, 11th March
item.description:
Zebra crossing markings all faded. Needs repainting. On queensway.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queensway, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Builders from 59 cadet drive left waste on main road, 11th March
item.description:
There was construction work going on at 59 cadet drive and a skip was present. However, the skip got too full so the waste was left next to it. When the skip was collected they did not collect the waste left on the road. Please arrange for enforcement of fly tipping as the builders should have removed their own waste after construction finished.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cadet Drive, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping by 135 Whinchat Road, 11th March
item.description:
Trolley of rubbish, mattresses, and household waste left down the side of property. This is a possible health and safety risk
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Nuthatch Gardens, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Massive pothole Essex Road/Britannia Row, 11th March
item.description:
Large pothole in southbound bus lane on Essex Road junction of Britannia Row, needs fixing urgently as it is causing houses nearby to shake as buses go over. Islington council is responsible for this as stated by TfL.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 64, Essex Road, Islington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish thrown out of the window, 11th March
item.description:
Maybe stop pretending you fix issues just to resolve reports without doing anything Https://reportaproblem.hackney.gov.uk/report/9082836

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Southwold Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
